channel.title: Signal — Praviveek Ray
channel.description: Daily AI and tech digest, research papers, and writing by a working data scientist.

item.title: AI & Tech Digest — July 22, 2026
item.description: Today's digest covers a significant security incident involving autonomous model behavior and new product updates across the AI landscape. OpenAI admits its models hacked Hugging Face on their own · OpenAI and Hugging Face partner to address security incident during model evaluation · Jack Dorsey launches Buzz to combine team chat, AI agents and Git hosting · OpenAI and Hugging Face address security incident during model evaluation · OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its pre-release models

item.title: AI & Tech Digest — July 21, 2026
item.description: Today's digest examines the intersection of geopolitical strategy, hardware innovation, and the persistent challenges of algorithmic bias and safety. OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be? · Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient · AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring · Safety and alignment in an era of long-horizon models · Who's afraid of Chinese models?

item.title: Paper: Toward Auditable Fraud Detection: Combining Graph Features, Model Explanations, and Agentic Case Investigation
item.description: This paper proposes a fraud detection framework that uses graph features and LLM-driven agents to explain and investigate suspicious transactions, helping teams build more transparent and accountable systems.

item.title: Paper: In-Context Time Series Classification with Random Convolutional Features
item.description: The authors show that combining random convolutional features with pretrained tabular foundation models improves time series classification, offering a robust alternative to traditional linear classifiers.

item.title: Paper: Assessment in Team Problem-Solving Exercises in Computing Education
item.description: This study explores using recorded team interaction data to automate and improve the assessment of collaborative problem-solving exercises, providing a path for more efficient performance evaluation.

item.title: Paper: Copy Less, Ground More: Overcoming Repetitive Copying in Long-Context Reasoning via Evidence-Aware Reinforcement Learning
item.description: The researchers introduce a reinforcement learning method to stop LLMs from mindlessly copying long-context inputs, forcing models to focus on relevant evidence for better reasoning performance.

item.title: AI & Tech Digest — July 20, 2026
item.description: Today's digest covers the intersection of hardware development, AI ethics, and the psychological impact of automated decision-making. Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans? · Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now · Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all · ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’ · How To use Claude's Reflect dashboard and learn when it's time to touch grass

item.title: Paper: SGA: Plug&Play Geometric Verification for Educational Video Synthesis
item.description: This tool automatically detects and fixes spatial errors in LLM-generated educational animations, ensuring that visual elements do not overlap incorrectly.

item.title: Paper: The Shared Discovery Paradox: How a One-Answer Rule Turns Better Information into Worse Search
item.description: This study reveals that sharing a single best-guess recommendation among a group can paradoxically reduce overall search success compared to decentralized, diverse exploration.

item.title: Paper: Natural Language Access to Domain-Specific Metadata: A Reusable Framework for LLM Query Generation
item.description: By mapping domain-specific metadata to an ontology, you can enable LLMs to generate accurate database queries without needing expensive fine-tuning or complex retrieval setups.

item.title: Paper: Vector Search As Nearest Neighbor Matching: RAG-based Policy Learning in Causal Inference
item.description: This framework treats RAG as a nearest-neighbor matching problem, allowing data scientists to optimize decision-making policies by retrieving and analyzing action-specific evidence.

item.title: The Week in AI — July 17, 2026
item.description: This week's developments reveal a pivot from raw model capability toward the messy, high-stakes realities of enterprise security, vertical specialization, and sustainable deployment. The Agentic Reality Gap · Verticalization Over Generalization · The Commoditization of Intelligence

item.title: AI & Tech Digest — July 17, 2026
item.description: Today's digest examines the intersection of AI safety, enterprise deployment challenges, and the growing commercialization of specialized foundation models. The Download: OpenAI unveils GPT-Red and heat pumps rise in the US · How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product · Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic · Why is OpenAI selling a ChatGPT basketball? · The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway

item.title: Paper: CRAFT: Clustering Rubrics to Diagnose Weak LLM Capabilities and Generate Targeted Fine-Tuning Data
item.description: The authors introduce a method to diagnose specific LLM weaknesses by analyzing rubric-based evaluations, helping practitioners generate targeted training data to fix model failures.

item.title: Paper: LLM-Powered Agentic AI for 5G/6G Networks: A Tutorial and Survey on Architectures, Protocols, and Standardization
item.description: This survey explores how agentic AI can automate 5G and 6G network management, providing a roadmap for integrating autonomous reasoning and planning into telecommunications infrastructure.

item.title: Paper: BayesPO: Bayesian Prompt Optimization via Parallel-Tempered Gradient-Guided Discrete MCMC
item.description: The researchers treat prompt optimization as a Bayesian sampling problem, offering a more rigorous mathematical approach to finding effective instructions without retraining model weights.

item.title: Paper: Knowledge-Guided Cross-Modal Fusion for Adult-to-Pediatric ECG Transfer via Label-Conditioned Contrastive Alignment
item.description: This framework improves pediatric ECG diagnosis by using knowledge-guided alignment to bridge the data gap between adult-heavy training sets and the unique requirements of pediatric patients.

item.title: Why I'm publishing this
item.description: A weekday AI digest, the papers I'm actually reading, and notes from building ML that touches 70M+ subscribers.

item.title: AI & Tech Digest — July 16, 2026
item.description: Today's updates focus on the shift from raw model development toward specialized security, enterprise integration, and competitive sales strategies. Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer · Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents · The Download: OpenAI unveils GPT-Red and heat pumps rise in the US · How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product · Anthropic, Blackstone bet the next trillion-dollar AI business is implementation, not just models

item.title: Paper: Digital Pantheon: Simulating and Auditing Coalition Formation with LLM Agents
item.description: This framework uses fine-tuning and retrieval to help LLM agents maintain consistent ideological stances, offering a way to simulate complex political negotiations without the usual neutrality bias.

item.title: Paper: SearchOS-V1: Towards Robust Open-Domain Information-Seeking Agent Collaboration
item.description: SearchOS improves information-seeking agents by using a persistent, shared state to prevent repetitive search loops, making automated web research more reliable and efficient.

item.title: Paper: Mutable Low-Rank Sketches for Retrain-Free Recommendation
item.description: This method uses mutable low-rank sketches to update user embeddings in real-time without full model retraining, significantly reducing staleness in recommendation systems.

item.title: Paper: Expanding the Lexicon of Ge'ez Based African Languages: A Comparative Study of Amharic and Tigrinya
item.description: By creating language-specific tokenizers for Ge'ez-script languages, this research improves the performance of multilingual models on low-resource African languages suffering from poor subword fragmentation.

item.title: Paper: Experience Memory Graph: One-Shot Error Correction for Agents
item.description: This method uses a graph-based memory structure to help AI agents fix errors in one shot, reducing the high costs and latency of iterative self-correction loops.

item.title: Paper: Unleashing Multimodal Large Language Models for Training-free HOI Detection in the Wild
item.description: The authors introduce a training-free approach for detecting human-object interactions that allows models to generalize to new scenarios without needing task-specific labeled data.

item.title: Paper: Screening of Biosecurity Features in Metagenomic Data with Evo 2 Probes
item.description: Researchers demonstrate that simple linear probes on genomic foundation models can effectively identify biosecurity threats like antimicrobial resistance without requiring expensive model fine-tuning.

item.title: Paper: AI-accelerated End-to-End Framework for Rapid Professional Upskilling
item.description: This framework integrates AI across the entire professional training lifecycle to significantly speed up content creation and skill assessment for enterprise upskilling.
